name: Editor
classification_notes: The typeface is a variable grotesque sans-serif with a wide range of weights from Thin to Black and an extensive set of OpenType alternates, consistent with a contemporary grotesque family.
summary: A variable grotesque with eight weights and an unusually deep set of OpenType stylistic alternates for fine-grained character-level control.
description: A variable grotesque spanning Thin to Black with a single-axis weight range and an interactive web editor for live preview. The OpenType feature set is notably broad, offering single and double storey 'a', alternate 'y', 'g', '&' and Greek phi, alongside contextual alternates, spaghetti alternates and headline letter variants. Greek character support extends the language coverage beyond standard Latin.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen Fonts present Editor as a variable grotesque family with eight named weights and a rich set of OpenType features, including stylistic sets for alternate letterforms, ligatures, contextual alternates and case-sensitive punctuation, previewed through an interactive web editor.

name: Noi Grotesk
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesk family drawing on Swiss typographic tradition; its structure, weight range and alternates place it firmly in the neo-grotesque category.
summary: A neo-grotesque family rooted in Swiss typographic tradition, distinguished by an unusually extensive set of OpenType alternates and full variable font support across eight weights.
description: Built on a Swiss grotesque foundation, Noi Grotesk introduces a friendlier tone through subtle detailing while offering thirteen stylistic sets covering alternate letterforms, headline variants, spaghetti alternates and more. The variable font axis spans Thin to Black with a continuous italic axis, giving designers fine-grained control over weight and slant. Broad Latin and Cyrillic language coverage makes it practical across multilingual projects.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen Fonts describe Noi Grotesk as a neo-grotesk family inspired by Swiss typographic traditions with a more contemporary and friendly character. They highlight its many features and alternates as making it surprisingly versatile, positioning it as a complex tool designed not just to solve everyday design challenges but to open up new ways of working with type.

name: Ease
classification_notes: Ease is a geometric sans-serif with multiple stylistic axes ranging from standard through increasingly rounded terminals to full-rounded and display variants, including a geometric alternates subfamily.
summary: A geometric sans with eight subfamilies spanning standard to fully rounded terminals and display cuts, available as a variable font.
description: Ease is structured around a single weight range of five cuts from Regular to Black, expressed across eight distinct stylistic families that progress from sharp standard geometry through increasingly soft rounded terminals to fully rounded and display variants. Two geometric alternates subfamilies, A and B, offer further divergence from the base forms. The entire range is available as variable fonts per subfamily, with the full family bundle including a single variable file covering all axes.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen offer Ease as a full family bundle discounted at 60%, covering eight subfamilies including Standard, SemiRounded, Rounded, DoubleRounded, FullRounded, SemiDisplay, Display and Geometric A+B, each available as variable fonts, with individual weights purchasable separately.

name: Studio Feixen Fonts
classification_notes: The page is a login/dashboard screen with no typeface content visible; category assigned as placeholder based on foundry identity.
summary: The provided URL resolves to a login dashboard rather than a specific typeface page, yielding no extractable font data.
description: The URL points to the Studio Feixen Fonts account dashboard, which requires authentication to access. No typeface name, weights, description or specimen content is available from this page. A specific typeface URL would be needed to produce a meaningful entry.
foundry_blurb: No foundry copy is available from this page, as it is a gated account dashboard requiring login credentials.

name: Ease
classification_notes: Ease presents clean, open letterforms consistent with geometric sans-serif conventions, with a friendly character appropriate to Studio Feixen's graphic design practice.
summary: A five-weight geometric sans by Felix Pfäffli and Robin Eberwein of Studio Feixen, running from Regular to Black.
description: Designed by Felix Pfäffli and Robin Eberwein, Ease sits within Studio Feixen's small in-house type library alongside Noi Grotesk and Studio Feixen Sans. Five weights from Regular to Black cover the range from running text to heavy display use, with consistent geometric proportions throughout. The family has no italic or condensed cuts, keeping it focused and uncluttered.
foundry_blurb: Ease is part of the Studio Feixen Fonts library, designed by Felix Pfäffli and Robin Eberwein and available in five weights: Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold and Black.

name: Noi Grotesk
classification_notes: A Swiss grotesque with humanist warmth, offering extensive OpenType alternates and variable font axes. The foundry frames it as sitting between Swiss tradition and friendliness.
summary: A Swiss grotesque with eight weights, a variable axis and an unusually rich set of OpenType alternates covering Latin, Cyrillic and Greek.
description: Designed by Robin Eberwein and Felix Pfäffli at Studio Feixen, Noi Grotesk 2.0 spans eight weights from Thin to Black with italics throughout, and ships in two variable configurations: one optimised for print and text, another for web and animation. The family carries over 1,500 glyphs and supports 281 languages across Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Modern Greek. Eleven stylistic sets cover everything from single-storey 'a' variants and creamy alternate forms to spaghetti letters and square dots, making it a grotesk with genuine typographic depth beyond its clean Swiss structure.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen describe Noi Grotesk as rooted in Swiss tradition with a sense of friendliness, following the principle that form follows beauty. Version 2.0 introduces a variable font in two flavours and expands the feature set with contextual alternates, discretionary ligatures and a broad language coverage of 281 languages.

name: Noi Grotesk
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesk family inspired by Swiss typographic traditions, placing it firmly in the neo-grotesque subcategory with contemporary inflections.
summary: A neo-grotesque from Studio Feixen Fonts that layers Swiss typographic discipline with a friendlier, more contemporary character and an unusually rich set of OpenType alternates.
description: Built as a variable font spanning eight named weights from Thin to Black, each with an italic axis, Noi Grotesk offers thirteen stylistic sets covering alternate letterforms, spaghetti variants and headline-specific treatments. Contextual alternates handle details automatically, such as turning 'x' into a multiplication sign between numerals or adjusting f and t based on surrounding characters. Latin and Cyrillic script support covers an extensive range of languages.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen Fonts describe Noi Grotesk as a neo-grotesk family inspired by Swiss typographic traditions but given a more contemporary and friendly touch. They emphasise its many features and alternates as making it surprisingly versatile, developed with the same questioning, boundary-pushing mindset that drives the studio's broader design work.
